purpose: Hard SPECTRA agent-ablation candidates where useful generalization axes are latent or require external scientific structure.
blacklist_pattern: Do not use capsules where the natural axis is directly exposed as a simple schema field, e.g. SMILES-only molecular novelty, mutation-position-only sequence novelty, or exact perturbation-component support.

candidates.hardness_reason: Useful axes require chemical structure, target/pathway annotations, donor/cell-state context, or baseline multiome priors rather than only aggregate error by cell type or drug name.
candidates.data_notes: Open Problems reports a 174.68 MiB DGE dataset; Hugging Face example is 682 MB. The source format includes SMILES, so the hard blinded capsule should expose only compound names/LINCS IDs and score whether agents decide to retrieve or construct chemical/pathway structure.

candidates.hardness_reason: Raw sequence strings do not reveal which motif families, TF programs, cell-type regulatory grammars, or variant-effect contexts should drive the audit.
candidates.data_notes: Code is public; processed task files and genome references are Synapse-hosted. A HOCOMOCO-derived motif-footprinting capsule is feasible without full Synapse execution.

candidates.hardness_reason: The hard setting should avoid exact combination component support and instead test unseen perturbations or biological states where pathway, network, drug, cytokine, or cell-line relatedness matters.
candidates.data_notes: Processed h5ad files are public on Hugging Face; Norman19 is approximately 395 MB compressed.

candidates.hardness_reason: The useful axes are physical data-generating parameters such as DFT fidelity, material structure/composition, signal curvature, and pileup/noise level.
candidates.data_notes: QMOF processed zip is approximately 138 MB; several Track Par-Label signal-shift archives are approximately 330-390 MB, while full Track archives are multi-GB.

candidates.hardness_reason: The shift is experimental replication over time and instruments; if split labels are hidden, agents must use acquisition/style/morphology structure rather than named test sets.
candidates.data_notes: Public Zenodo hdf5 files total roughly 4.3 GB across train and four test sets.

candidates.hardness_reason: Useful if metadata labels are hidden and agents must infer batch, site, scanner, or staining/style axes from images or embeddings.
candidates.data_notes: WILDS reports 7 GB download for RxRx1 and 10 GB for Camelyon17.

candidates.hardness_reason: The meaningful audit axes combine chemical structure, MoA hierarchy, concentration, morphology profiles, and plate effects rather than only compound identity.
candidates.data_notes: Hugging Face mirror reports 45.7 GB raw images; smaller precomputed morphology profiles should be found before using this as an ablation.

candidates.hardness_reason: Low-friction control for tabular generalization, but weaker than biology/physics candidates unless site, time, or population axes are hidden and must be constructed.
candidates.data_notes: TableShift includes public datasets and standard train/validation/id_test/ood_test splits.
